Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Sep-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Colin Jones and Daniel B. Miller of Wilshire Law Firm LLP (213-381-9988) appear as counsels for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Sonja M. Dahl of Donnelly Nelson DePolo & Murray (925-287-8181) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Queen of the Valley Medical Center

Attorney, Justin Muro of Muro & Lampe, Inc. (916-817-6300) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant(s)/non-opposing party, Blakeslee Electric, et al.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT QUEEN OF THE VALLEY MEDICAL CENTER'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damages allegations from Plaintiff's Complaint is DENIED. At this early stage of the case, the real issue is whether plaintiff should be precluded from taking discovery directed at whether he can obtain evidence warranting the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to allow him to do so. If, however, after plaintiff has had a reasonable opportunity to seek facts supporting punitive damages and defendant still believes there are no such facts, defendant's recourse is to file a motion for summary adjudication. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
